page: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. This configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and includes 480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are included.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, due to the limited physical core count, multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory read bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10% for small cached blocks and the bottom 25% for large uncached blocks, though write bandwidth remains average.

Given these performance characteristics, the L2as_v4 is suited for workloads that benefit from high single-thread performance but do not require massive parallel processing. While it features a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local storage, its database performance is constrained, as indicated by poor database operation scores and weak Redis SET throughput. Consequently, it is best utilized for light database caching, single-threaded file processing, or low-traffic web serving where single-core speed is prioritized over multi-threaded throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks. However, its multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are limited, placing in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. The server offers a qualitative balance of high single-thread speed and moderate memory capacity. It is best suited for workloads prioritizing single-core processing over heavy parallelization, such as light web serving, single-threaded data processing, and basic database tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is a virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage, offering strong single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L2as_v4 is a virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A dual-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, 16 GB of RAM, and local storage for single-thread intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory paired with 480 GB of local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Limited multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth
Well-suited for single-threaded processing and light database tasks
